title: Direct Triboelectric Programming of a Ferroelectric Synaptic Transistor for Neuromorphic Tactile Perception
pdf_version_label: 暂无可合法自动获取的开放全文

abstract.original: ABSTRACT Triboelectric nanogenerators provide a direct electrical interface to mechanical stimuli, yet most triboelectric–neuromorphic integrations rely on volatile conductance modulation or indirect capacitive coupling, limiting memory‐assisted tactile perception. Here, we demonstrate direct triboelectric programming of a MoS 2 /HfO 2 /PbZr 0.3 Ti 0.7 O 3 ferroelectric transistor, where rectified triboelectric pulses are coupled to ferroelectric switching to realize cumulative conductance modulation. The spike‐like outputs generated by mechanical contact–separation are translated into history‐dependent conductance evolution through polarization‐controlled electrostatic modulation in PbZr 0.3 Ti 0.7 O 3 assisted by charge trapping in HfO 2 . Systematic correlation between triboelectric pulse amplitude, repetition frequency, and channel conductance reveals that repetitive mechanical inputs progressively accumulate polarization‐mediated states without external signal amplification or continuous waveform processing. The device exhibits fundamental synaptic characteristics under electrical stimulation, while triboelectrically generated inputs enable cumulative tactile programming. By constructing a force–frequency‐dependent conductance map and defining a reference current window, tactile conditions are encoded into stable state regimes, allowing threshold‐based discrimination based on accumulated device states rather than instantaneous signals. Furthermore, the experimentally parameterized encoding framework is extended to electrocardiogram signal classification, demonstrating its capability to transform temporal bio‐signals into device‐relevant conductance features for neuromorphic signal processing. These results establish a physics‐grounded route for analog memory‐assisted tactile state encoding in neuromorphic sensory systems.
abstract.zh: 该工作用摩擦电接触-分离产生的脉冲直接编程 MoS2/HfO2/PbZr0.3Ti0.7O3 铁电晶体管。整流后的摩擦电脉冲触发铁电极化切换，并与 HfO2 电荷俘获共同形成随刺激历史累积的电导状态，因此无需外部信号放大或连续波形处理即可把重复机械输入编码为模拟记忆。作者进一步建立力-频率-电导映射和参考电流窗口，用累积状态而非瞬时信号区分触觉条件，并把实验参数化编码框架扩展到心电信号分类。当前结论来自摘要级核验，尚未获得合法开放全文。

innovation_points: 把摩擦电脉冲与铁电极化切换直接耦合，在单个晶体管内实现机械刺激驱动的累积电导调制，而不是依赖易失电导或间接电容耦合。
用力和重复频率共同控制稳定电导状态，并通过参考电流窗口完成阈值判别，使触觉识别依赖刺激历史而非单次瞬时幅值。
在无外部放大和连续波形处理的条件下完成传感-记忆-编码，并用心电分类展示该编码机制对其他时序信号的可迁移性。
inspirations: 在你的 ADC 前物理投影中增加“累积态”通道：除 Kz/Kx/Ky 瞬时特征外，设计对接触次数、频率或滑动事件历史敏感的模拟状态。
建立 instantaneous readout 与 accumulated-state encoding 对照，比较纹理/滑移任务精度、所需采样率、ADC 通道数和后端模型复杂度。
该摘要证明的是单器件触觉记忆编码，不等同于阵列级矢量前端；需要额外验证器件离散、状态漂移、擦写循环和跨器件阈值迁移。
notes: 当前仅完成题录/摘要级筛选。
